agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Integration of Warehouse Management (Context A) and Sales/Order Management (Context B) to enable real-time stock availability checks at order placement time. This is a cross-bounded-context coupling request that fundamentally changes the autonomy of the Sales context.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration: Warehouse Management ↔ Sales/Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Product","definition":"An item managed in Warehouse context with a StockLevel; referenced by ID in Sales context. Each context owns its own projection of 'Product'."},{"term":"StockLevel","definition":"Warehouse-owned integer (>= 0) representing current physical inventory of a Product. Authoritative source of stock truth."},{"term":"Order","definition":"Sales-owned aggregate representing a customer's purchase intent, containing one or more OrderLines."},{"term":"OrderLine","definition":"A line within an Order referencing a Product ID and a requested Quantity."},{"term":"Stock Reservation","definition":"A tentative claim on inventory that reduces available stock without yet triggering a physical pick. Essential concept currently MISSING from both contexts."},{"term":"Available Stock","definition":"StockLevel minus all outstanding (unpicked) reservations. This is the correct quantity to check for new orders — NOT raw StockLevel."},{"term":"Stock Available Indicator","definition":"A read-model projection in the Sales context showing near-real-time availability. Must be understood as eventually consistent, not transactionally accurate."},{"term":"Blocked Order","definition":"An Order that has been rejected or held because available stock is insufficient to fulfill one or more OrderLines at placement time."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"StockLevel in Warehouse context must never go below zero.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — you cannot ship items that do not exist. Violating this corrupts inventory data and causes fulfillment failures.","testable_assertion":"For every Product, StockLevel >= 0 at all times, including under concurrent operations."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An Order must be blocked if, at placement time, Available Stock (not raw StockLevel) for any referenced Product is less than the requested Quantity on any OrderLine.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"This is a business policy the sales team wants enforced. A VP could decide to allow backorders or partial fulfillment instead.","testable_assertion":"Given Product X has Available Stock of 5, when an Order is placed with Quantity 6 for Product X, then the Order is blocked."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Warehouse context is the sole authority on stock truth. Sales context must NOT own or mutate stock data — it consumes a read projection.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"If two contexts independently track stock, data will diverge and inventory integrity is destroyed. Warehouse is the single source of truth for physical inventory.","testable_assertion":"No write to StockLevel or reservation state originates from the Sales bounded context directly; all mutations go through Warehouse context commands or domain events."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"A Stock Reservation must be created when an Order passes the availability check, and released if the Order is cancelled before picking.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Without reservations, two orders can each pass the availability check against the same stock, causing overselling. This is a data consistency invariant.","testable_assertion":"Given Product X has StockLevel 10 and 0 reservations, when Order A reserves 7 and Order B attempts to reserve 5, then Order B is blocked because Available Stock is 3."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"The 'stock available' indicator shown in the Sales context is eventually consistent and must NOT be used as the authoritative gate for blocking orders.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Using a stale read model as the transactional decision point would allow orders to slip through during propagation delay, violating BR-1 downstream. The blocking decision must query Warehouse authoritatively.","testable_assertion":"The order-blocking decision invokes a synchronous command/query to the Warehouse context (or its reservation service), not the local read model cache."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"The 'stock available' indicator must reflect changes within an acceptable staleness window (business-defined SLA, e.g., seconds not minutes).","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The exact freshness SLA is a business preference, not a physical constraint. It could be tuned based on sales volume and operational needs.","testable_assertion":"After a StockLevel change event is published by Warehouse, the Sales read model is updated within the configured SLA threshold."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A Product has Available Stock of N in the Warehouse context","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for that Product with Quantity <= N","then":"The Order is accepted, and a Stock Reservation of that Quantity is created in the Warehouse context, reducing Available Stock accordingly"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A Product has Available Stock of N in the Warehouse context","when":"A customer places an Order with an OrderLine for that Product with Quantity > N","then":"The Order is blocked, no reservation is created, and the customer/sales team receives a clear reason indicating insufficient stock"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"Two customers simultaneously place orders that individually fit within Available Stock but collectively exceed it","when":"Both orders are processed","then":"Exactly one order is accepted and one is blocked; StockLevel never goes below zero; no overselling occurs"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An accepted Order is cancelled before items are picked","when":"The cancellation is processed","then":"The Stock Reservation is released, and Available Stock increases by the reserved quantity"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A Product's StockLevel changes in the Warehouse context (receipt or pick)","when":"The change event propagates to the Sales read model","then":"The 'stock available' indicator updates within the configured staleness SLA"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"The Warehouse context is temporarily unreachable","when":"A customer places an Order","then":"The Order is NOT silently accepted; the system either queues the order for retry or rejects it with a clear unavailability message — it must not bypass the stock check"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An Order contains multiple OrderLines for different Products","when":"Any single OrderLine exceeds Available Stock for its Product","then":"The entire Order is blocked (atomic check), not partially accepted"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Concurrent orders for the last unit of stock","expected_behavior":"Only one order succeeds. The reservation mechanism must use optimistic concurrency or locking to prevent double-allocation. The losing order is blocked."},{"scenario":"Stock received (supplier delivery) while an order is being evaluated","expected_behavior":"If reservation uses a consistent snapshot, the new stock may not be visible to the in-flight check. The order may be blocked and customer can retry. This is acceptable — better to under-promise than oversell."},{"scenario":"Warehouse context is down or network partition","expected_behavior":"Sales context must NOT fall back to the stale read model for the blocking decision. Orders should be held/queued or rejected with a clear message. Silent acceptance would violate BR-1 downstream."},{"scenario":"Order cancelled after picking has begun","expected_behavior":"Reservation cannot be released — the pick has consumed the stock. Cancellation rules differ post-pick and must be handled by Warehouse context, not Sales."},{"scenario":"Product exists in Sales catalog but not yet in Warehouse system","expected_behavior":"Order should be blocked with a clear 'product not stocked' reason, not treated as zero stock (which is ambiguous)."},{"scenario":"StockLevel is zero but a supplier delivery is expected tomorrow","expected_behavior":"The system blocks the order based on current Available Stock. Future expected receipts do not count unless an explicit 'backorder' policy (outside current scope) is implemented."},{"scenario":"Read model shows stock available but reservation fails","expected_behavior":"The read model was stale. The order is blocked. The indicator should update shortly. The customer sees a clear message, not a generic error."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Prevents overselling, which causes costly order cancellations, emergency procurement, refunds, and customer compensation. However, overly aggressive blocking could suppress valid sales if the reservation/read-model system is poorly tuned (false negatives from stale data).","operational":"Introduces a synchronous cross-context dependency at order placement — a significant architectural coupling. Warehouse context availability now directly affects Sales throughput. Requires new infrastructure: reservation tracking, event-based read model projection, and monitoring of propagation lag.","user":"Sales team gains real-time visibility into stock, reducing wasted effort on unfulfillable orders. Customers get immediate feedback instead of post-placement rejection. However, the eventually-consistent indicator may cause confusion when it shows 'available' but the order is blocked (stale read model).","risk":"Primary risk is introducing tight coupling between two previously independent contexts. If the integration is implemented as a direct synchronous call from Sales to Warehouse, Warehouse downtime halts all order placement. Secondary risk: the Reservation concept does not yet exist in either context — this is net-new domain modeling that must be carefully designed to avoid corrupting the StockLevel invariant.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Faster feedback on order viability; potential frustration if indicator shows available but order is blocked due to staleness"},{"group":"Sales Team","impact":"Real-time stock visibility improves planning; blocked orders add a new workflow state they must handle (retry, notify customer, suggest alternatives)"},{"group":"Warehouse Operations","impact":"Must now support reservation lifecycle (create, release, convert-to-pick); increased write load and new failure modes"},{"group":"Engineering / Platform","impact":"Must design and maintain cross-context integration (events, read models, reservation service); new operational monitoring for propagation lag and availability SLAs"},{"group":"Finance / Revenue","impact":"Reduced overselling losses; must monitor for revenue impact from false blocks (orders rejected due to stale data or reservation contention)"}]},"confidence":{"level":52,"reasoning":"The feature request is underspecified in several critical areas: (1) no mention of the Reservation concept, which is essential to correctness, (2) 'automatically block' is ambiguous — does it mean reject outright or hold for review? (3) 'real time' indicator has no defined staleness SLA, (4) no guidance on what happens when Warehouse is unreachable, (5) no policy on partial fulfillment vs all-or-nothing. The spec conflates a read-model concern (indicator) with a transactional concern (blocking) without acknowledging they require fundamentally different consistency guarantees.","high_confidence_areas":["StockLevel >= 0 is a hard invariant","Warehouse must remain the sole authority on stock","A reservation concept is required to prevent overselling under concurrency","The read-model indicator and the blocking decision require different consistency models"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether 'block' means reject or hold for manual review","Partial fulfillment policy (block entire order vs. individual lines)","Staleness SLA for the stock indicator","Behavior when Warehouse context is unavailable","Whether backorders should be supported"],"assumptions":["'Block' means the order is rejected at placement time, not held in a pending queue","The blocking check is all-or-nothing across all OrderLines in the Order","No backorder capability exists or is desired","The Reservation concept is new and does not yet exist in the Warehouse context"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The term 'automatically block' is undefined. Does it mean (A) reject the order outright and inform the customer, or (B) place the order in a 'held' state for sales team review? These have very different domain models, UX, and reservation semantics.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Customers","Engineering"],"options":["(A) Reject immediately with reason — simpler, clearer, but potentially frustrating for customers","(B) Hold for review — more flexible, but requires a new 'Held' order state and manual workflow"],"recommendation":"(A) Reject immediately for v1. A 'Held' state adds significant complexity and should be a separate feature if needed."},{"type":"missing_context","description":"The Reservation concept is completely absent from both bounded contexts but is REQUIRED to prevent overselling under concurrent orders. Without it, the feature cannot be implemented correctly. The team needs to decide: does the Reservation live in the Warehouse context (recommended) or in a new shared context?","affected_stakeholders":["Warehouse Operations","Engineering","Finance"],"options":["Reservation as part of Warehouse aggregate","Reservation as a new dedicated bounded context","No reservation — accept race condition risk"],"recommendation":"Reservation belongs in the Warehouse context as it directly modifies Available Stock, which is derived from StockLevel (Warehouse-owned data)."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"'Real time' stock indicator has no defined consistency SLA. True real-time would require synchronous queries to Warehouse on every page load, which conflicts with bounded context autonomy. Eventually consistent via events is standard but 'real time' sets incorrect expectations.","affected_stakeholders":["Sales Team","Engineering","Product"],"options":["(A) Event-driven projection with sub-second SLA — near-real-time, scalable","(B) Synchronous API call to Warehouse on each view — true real-time but tight coupling","(C) Polling-based refresh — simple but wasteful"],"recommendation":"(A) Event-driven projection. Define 'real time' as 'within 2 seconds of change' in the SLA and communicate this to the sales team."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"No policy defined for partial fulfillment. If an Order has 3 lines and only 1 lacks stock, should the entire Order be blocked or should 2 lines be accepted?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Sales Team","Warehouse Operations"],"options":["(A) All-or-nothing — entire order blocked if any line fails","(B) Partial acceptance — fulfill what's available, block the rest"],"recommendation":"(A) All-or-nothing for v1. Partial fulfillment introduces split-shipment logistics, partial payment, and significant domain complexity."}],"rejection_reasons":[{"violation":"Feature request implies Sales context should have direct access to stock data for the real-time indicator, which would violate bounded context boundaries","business_rule_broken":"BR-3: Warehouse is sole authority on stock truth"},{"violation":"Without introducing the Reservation concept, any implementation will allow concurrent orders to oversell — passing availability checks independently against the same stock","business_rule_broken":"BR-4: Stock Reservation is required to prevent double-allocation"}]}

This is a cross-bounded-context coupling request that fundamentally changes the autonomy of the Sales context.","bounded_context":"Cross-context integration: Warehouse Management ↔ Sales/Order Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Product","definition":"An item managed in Warehouse context with
